description:
lib.rs:363-371 — The SigningBackend trait contract (secretx-core line 1411) specifies:

    Return the public key as DER-encoded SubjectPublicKeyInfo.

But the TPM2 implementation marshals the raw TPMT_PUBLIC structure and returns those bytes. These are not SPKI DER. Any caller that passes the result to an X.509 library or DER parser will get garbage.

The comment on line 363 even acknowledges this: "Marshal the TPM2B_PUBLIC structure. Callers can parse the algorithm-specific fields from the marshalled bytes." — this is documenting a contract violation, not fixing it.

Fix: after reading the TPM public area, extract the algorithm-specific key material (ECC point or RSA modulus+exponent) and encode it as proper SubjectPublicKeyInfo using a DER encoder (e.g. spki or der crate). This is what the AWS KMS and local-signing backends presumably do.
title: public_key_der returns TPM2B_PUBLIC, not SubjectPublicKeyInfo DER
